task_context.actionable_question: Am I a procrastinator and is it costing me marks?
task_context.ai_prompt_hint: Use submission_delay_avg [FE]. Identify if late submission is systematic. Link to score.

explanation.raw_text:
Summary: You've shown a pattern of submitting your assessments late, which could be impacting your scores. While you are passing, the delays have increased over time, suggesting a need to address procrastination.

Insights: Increasing Submission Delays: Your average submission delay is 3.25 days, and it has increased from 3 days for the first assessment to 5 days for the last one. This trend indicates a growing tendency to procrastinate. | Score Correlation with Delays: As your submission delays have increased, your scores have decreased, with the last assessment scoring 83 compared to 100 on the first. This suggests that timely submissions may positively impact your performance.

Educational implications: Timely submissions are crucial for maintaining high scores and overall engagement in your studies.

Recommendations: high - Set specific deadlines for yourself before the actual due dates. - This can help you manage your time better and reduce the tendency to procrastinate. | medium - Break down assessments into smaller tasks and schedule them. - This approach can make the workload feel more manageable and help you stay on track.

explanation.summary: You've shown a pattern of submitting your assessments late, which could be impacting your scores. While you are passing, the delays have increased over time, suggesting a need to address procrastination.

insights.title: Increasing Submission Delays
insights.description: Your average submission delay is 3.25 days, and it has increased from 3 days for the first assessment to 5 days for the last one. This trend indicates a growing tendency to procrastinate.

insights.title: Score Correlation with Delays
insights.description: As your submission delays have increased, your scores have decreased, with the last assessment scoring 83 compared to 100 on the first. This suggests that timely submissions may positively impact your performance.

educational_implications: Timely submissions are crucial for maintaining high scores and overall engagement in your studies.

recommendations.action: Set specific deadlines for yourself before the actual due dates.
recommendations.rationale: This can help you manage your time better and reduce the tendency to procrastinate.

recommendations.action: Break down assessments into smaller tasks and schedule them.
recommendations.rationale: This approach can make the workload feel more manageable and help you stay on track.

confidence.reason: The data shows clear trends in submission delays and scores.

required_core_outputs.description: Identify whether late submission is systematic.
Describe the observed relationship between submission delay and score.

evaluation_constraints.description: Use submission_delay_avg as the primary delay metric; do not infer procrastination from score alone.
Frame the delay-score relationship as correlational, not causal.

evaluation_requirements.safety_fairness_note: Conservative pilot default; human review is required before any not_applicable exception.
